en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A SONG. A PSALM. Shout for joy to God, all the earth;
zh: 全地的居民哪！ 你們應當向 神歡呼 。

en: sing the glory of his name; give to him glorious praise!
zh: 你們要歌頌他名的榮耀， 把榮耀和讚美都歸給他。

en: Say to God, "How awesome are your deeds! So great is your power that your enemies come cringing to you.
zh: 要對 神說：“你的作為多麼可畏！ 因你偉大的能力，你的仇敵必向你假意歸順。

en: All the earth worships you and sings praises to you; they sing praises to your name." Selah
zh: 全地的居民都必敬拜你， 向你歌頌， 歌頌你的名。”

en: Come and see what God has done: he is awesome in his deeds toward the children of man.
zh: 你們來看 神的作為， 他向世人所作的事是可畏懼的。

en: He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the river on foot. There did we rejoice in him,
zh: 他把海變為乾地， 眾人就步行過河， 我們要在那裡因他歡喜。

en: who rules by his might forever, whose eyes keep watch on the nations- let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah
zh: 他以自己的權能永遠統管萬有， 他的眼睛鑒察萬國， 悖逆的人不可自高。

en: Bless our God, O peoples; let the sound of his praise be heard,
zh: 萬民哪！你們要稱頌我們的 神， 宣揚讚美他的聲音。

en: who has kept our soul among the living and has not let our feet slip.
zh: 他保全我們的性命， 不使我們的腳動搖。

en: For you, O God, have tested us; you have tried us as silver is tried.
zh: 神啊！你試煉了我們， 熬煉了我們，如同熬煉銀子一般。

en: You brought us into the net; you laid a crushing burden on our backs;
zh: 你使我們進入網羅， 把重擔放在我們的背上。

en: you let men ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water; yet you have brought us out to a place of abundance.
zh: 你使人駕車軋我們的頭， 我們經過水火， 你卻把我們領到豐盛之地。

en: I will come into your house with burnt offerings; I will perform my vows to you,
zh: 我要帶著燔祭進你的殿， 我要向你還我的願，

en: that which my lips uttered and my mouth promised when I was in trouble.
zh: 就是在急難的時候， 我嘴唇所許的，我口裡所說的。

en: I will offer to you burnt offerings of fattened animals, with the smoke of the sacrifice of rams; I will make an offering of bulls and goats. Selah
zh: 我要把肥美的牲畜作燔祭， 連同公綿羊的馨香祭獻給你， 我並且要把公牛和公山羊獻上。

en: Come and hear, all you who fear God, and I will tell what he has done for my soul.
zh: 所有敬畏 神的人哪！ 你們都要來聽， 我要述說他為我所行的事。

en: I cried to him with my mouth, and high praise was on my tongue.
zh: 我曾用口向他呼求， 我曾用舌頭頌讚他。

en: If I had cherished iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.
zh: 如果我心裡偏向罪孽， 主必不聽；

en: But truly God has listened; he has attended to the voice of my prayer.
zh: 神卻垂聽了， 他留心聽了我禱告的聲音。

en: Blessed be God, because he has not rejected my prayer or removed his steadfast love from me!
zh: 神是應當稱頌的， 他沒有拒絕我的禱告， 也沒有使他的慈愛離開我。
